architecture_fit.note: Clean 2-layer/8-head pre-LN transformer, small delta from base_model.py; solves the measured task but training directly maximizes the scored quantity and 2 layers/MLP not ablated for minimality.

baseline_comparison.note: Relies on the benchmark's built-in uniform 1/seq_len baseline (fidelity 12.35x); no deliberate strawman like an untrained or no-constraint-loss ablation.

faithfulness.note: No causal/ablation/patching evidence and no downstream task whose behavior could break; attention is optimized straight to the metric and README proposes no faithfulness check.

operating_range.note: Full distance sweep 1->16 reported; fidelity stays flat ~12x with no decay or silent failure, though range is ~1.2 orders and seq_len is not varied.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: N/A — no hand-built circuit; weights are learned.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab is broken: main.py never saves model.pt and app.py loads it from a fresh results_dir, so table+heatmap always show 'No trained model found'; only the Benchmark panel works.

visualisation_rationale.note: README's 'Why this visualisation' clearly ties the table+heatmap to the distance-decay question, but the described interactive viz does not actually function.

notes: A trained 2-layer transformer cleanly hits 12.35x fidelity at the canonical distance and, interestingly, holds nearly flat across all sweep distances with no decay. But the result is weakened by teaching-to-the-test (the loss maximizes the exact metric) with no faithfulness/causal evidence, and the headline interactive Demo is broken because no checkpoint is ever saved. Mean of scored items ~3.0 -> borderline.
